team.name: user_071
team.contact: Operator who installed PM-00005 on PRD-00461

team.name: user_089
team.contact: Rework technician who diagnosed and repaired DEF-00098

doc.problem: Gateway Module GM-400 (PRD-00461, order PO-00020) failed to power on at visual inspection (SEC-00001 / Montage Linie 1). Fuse blew immediately on power-up. Defect classified as MISSING_PART (high severity) with reported part PM-00005 (LED 5mm rot) at BOM position LED. Occurrence traced to Montage Linie 2 (SEC-00010). Part P-000226 from supplier batch SB-00005 (PartStream Industries, received 2025-10-12) was installed by user_071 on 2026-03-26. Rework RW-00088 by user_089: re-soldered the DC jack (dry joints found), tested continuity — 21 min, cost €57.96. Total defect cost: €148.94.

inProduction.reference: PO-00020: 9 units total, 1 defective (PRD-00461). Remaining 8 units (PRD-00431, PRD-00445, PRD-00462, PRD-00465, PRD-00470, PRD-00473, PRD-00485, PRD-00488) — none shipped.

inTransit.reference: No units from PO-00020 have been shipped per product_action records.

atCustomer.reference: No units from PO-00020 shipped to customer.

production_stop.description: Hold remaining 8 units from PO-00020 at Montage Linie 2. Perform power-on continuity check on all DC jack solder joints before releasing.

customer_informed.description: Internal defect — no units shipped. Customer notification not required at this stage.

internal_info.description: Notify Montage Linie 2 team leads of dry solder joint finding on DC jack. Emphasize visual check of solder joints before unit moves downstream.

sample_request.description: Defective unit PRD-00461 retained in-house after rework (RW-00088). No external sample needed.

warehouse_sort.description: Sort all PO-00020 units held in warehouse/WIP for DC jack solder joint integrity. Power-on test each unit.

derogation.description: Not applicable — defect is safety-critical (fuse blown). No derogation permissible.

sub_supplier_claim.description: Root cause points to assembly process (dry solder), not incoming material defect. Sub-supplier claim against PartStream Industries (SB-00005) not warranted at this stage.

additional_controls.description: Introduce temporary 100% power-on test at Montage Linie 2 (SEC-00010) exit gate for all GM-400 units until corrective action is verified.

other.description: Re-classify DEF-00098 defect code from MISSING_PART to SOLDER_COLD or CONNECTION_OPEN to correct the trend data.

occurrence.potentialCause: LED (PM-00005) installed at BOM position LED by user_071, but rework revealed dry solder joints on the DC jack — suggesting an assembly process failure at Montage Linie 2 (SEC-00010). The defect code MISSING_PART may be a misclassification; the symptom (fuse blown immediately, no power) is more consistent with a solder/connection defect on the power path than a literally missing LED.
whys: Why did the fuse blow? — Short circuit or excessive current draw at power-on due to dry solder joints on the DC jack.
Why were the DC jack joints dry? — Insufficient solder application or wrong soldering temperature profile at SEC-00010.
Why was the soldering inadequate? — Operator may not have followed the work instruction for hand-soldering the DC jack, or the soldering iron tip was worn.
Why was this not caught earlier? — No in-process electrical continuity check between Montage Linie 2 and downstream inspection.
Why is the defect coded MISSING_PART? — Visual inspection at SEC-00001 saw the non-illuminated LED and coded it as missing; root cause is actually a solder/connection defect upstream.

rootCauses.text: Dry solder joints on DC jack at Montage Linie 2 (SEC-00010) causing short/open circuit and fuse blow on power-up
Possible work instruction non-compliance or soldering tool degradation at SEC-00010

detection.potentialCause: Defect was detected at visual inspection (SEC-00001, Montage Linie 1) only when the unit failed to power on. There is no in-process electrical test after Montage Linie 2 assembly that would catch dry solder joints before the unit reaches final inspection.
whys: Why was the defect not detected at SEC-00010? — No in-process continuity or power-on test is performed at Montage Linie 2 after soldering.
Why is there no in-process test? — The control plan for GM-400 may not include a post-solder electrical check at this station.
Why wasn't this gap identified before? — This is the only MISSING_PART defect for ART-00005 at SEC-00010, so the gap was not visible in trend data.
Why did visual inspection at SEC-00001 misclassify it? — The LED not lighting was interpreted as a missing part rather than a connection defect.
Why is misclassification a problem? — Incorrect defect coding obscures the true root cause and delays corrective action.

rootCauses.text: No in-process electrical verification after hand-soldering at Montage Linie 2 (SEC-00010)
Defect code misclassification at visual inspection — MISSING_PART instead of SOLDER_COLD or CONNECTION_OPEN

plannedOccurrence.description: Review and update soldering work instruction for DC jack at Montage Linie 2 (SEC-00010). Include solder temperature profile, minimum solder fill criteria, and visual acceptance standard for through-hole joints.
Inspect and recalibrate/replace soldering iron tips at SEC-00010. Establish a tip replacement schedule (preventive maintenance).

plannedDetection.description: Add in-process power-on / continuity test at SEC-00010 exit gate for GM-400 units. Update control plan accordingly.
Retrain visual inspection operators at SEC-00001 on defect code classification: distinguish MISSING_PART from SOLDER_COLD / CONNECTION_OPEN symptoms.

doc.otherPartsWhich: N/A — root cause is assembly process at SEC-00010, not a part defect. However, any through-hole soldered connector at Montage Linie 2 could be affected by the same process gap.

implementedDetection.note: Update once the in-process test at SEC-00010 is operational and operator retraining at SEC-00001 is complete. Record date, effectiveness rating, and notes.

preventive.other.note: Issue a classification quick-reference card distinguishing MISSING_PART from SOLDER_COLD/CONNECTION_OPEN at visual inspection.

champion.note: Enter the management sponsor / champion for this 8D (typically Quality Manager or Plant Manager).

coordinator.note: Enter the Quality Engineer coordinating this 8D investigation.

team.note: Replace user IDs and role placeholders with actual names. Add Process Engineering and Test Engineering representatives.

appreciation.note: Complete after all corrective actions are implemented and effectiveness is confirmed (target: zero recurrence over 4 weeks). Include lessons learned, team recognition, and management sign-off date.
